2606.17786.title: Toward Accessible Psychotherapy Training Using AI-Driven Interactive Patient Avatars
2606.17786.paper_summary: Training psychotherapists in evidence-based interventions such as Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) requires repeated practice with meaningful feedback, yet opportunities for safe, standardized training are limited by ethical, logistical, and resource constraints. We introduce a system designed to support ACT-oriented psychotherapy training through spoken dialogue with an embodied virtual patient. The system uses large language models to simulate patient behavior conditioned on profiles derived from real therapy sessions and configurable clinical scenarios, while a separate automated evaluator provides turn-by-turn feedback on therapist responses based on established ACT fidelity criteria. Rather than aiming to replace supervision, the system is intended to support deliberate practice by enabling experimentation, reflection, and immediate feedback in low-risk settings. Expert evaluation with practicing psychologists confirmed high realism in patient behavior and demonstrated that immediate turn-by-turn ACT feedback increased therapists' awareness of intervention choices and enabled effective experimentation with alternative responses. Quantitative evaluation across 49 therapy transcripts identified GPT-4o-mini as the optimal feedback model, achieving the lowest mean absolute error (MAE = 6.12) in replicating human supervisor ACT fidelity ratings with statistically significant agreement. This work demonstrates the potential of fidelity-aware simulated patients as a scalable complement to psychotherapy training.
2606.17786.paper_summary_zh: 訓練心理治療師進行基於證據的介入，例如接受與承諾療法（ACT），需要重複練習並獲得有意義的反饋，但由於倫理、後勤和資源限制，安全、標準化訓練的機會有限。<lb/>我們介紹了一個系統，旨在通過與具身虛擬病人的口語對話來支持以ACT為導向的心理治療訓練。<lb/>該系統使用大型語言模型來模擬患者行為，這些行為是基於從真實治療會話中衍生的個人檔案和可配置的臨床場景，而一個獨立的自動評估器則根據既定的ACT忠實度標準提供逐步反饋。<lb/>該系統的目的是支持有意識的練習，通過使實驗、反思和在低風險環境中獲得即時反饋成為可能，而不是取代監督。<lb/>與執業心理學家的專家評估確認了患者行為的高真實感，並顯示即時逐步的ACT反饋提高了治療師對介入選擇的認識，並使他們能夠有效地實驗替代反應。<lb/>對49份治療記錄的定量評估確定了GPT-4o-mini為最佳反饋模型，實現了在複製人類監督者ACT忠實度評分時最低的平均絕對誤差（MAE = 6.12），並具有統計學上顯著的一致性。<lb/>這項工作展示了忠實度意識的模擬患者作為心理治療訓練可擴展補充的潛力。
2606.17786.author: Pascal Riachi et.al.
2606.17786.authors: Pascal Riachi, Sofie Kamber, Stella Brogna, Andrew Gloster, Rafael Wampfler
